summary: Truncated integer cube root on u64 via a three-way branch dispatch (`a < 8` constant, `a ≤ u32::MAX` Hacker's-Delight icbrt2, `a > u32::MAX` integer-guess + cubic Newton); composes FIVE tail-recursive helpers (cbrt_u32_loop icbrt2 kernel, log2_floor_rec, pow2_loop, fixpoint_cbrt_up, fixpoint_cbrt_down) — the icbrt2 arm is structurally distinct from Newton: a bit-by-bit reconstruction threading FOUR invariants `y2 = y² ∧ y³·8^s_iter ≤ a_orig ∧ a_orig < (y+1)³·8^s_iter ∧ x = a_orig − y³·8^s_iter` through ~20 monadic-bind / overflow-discharge steps per iteration, with the loop body proved via the key cubic identity `(2y+1)³ = 8y³ + b` (where `b = 12y² + 6y + 1` is the body's test threshold) and a `Nat.le_div_iff`-shape bridge `(x >>> s) ≥ b ↔ b·2^s ≤ x`, every u32 step's overflow discharged from the bit-width bound `y < 2^11` derived from `y³·8^s_iter ≤ a < 2^32`; the Newton arm is the cubic analog of `sqrt_u64_modified`, with the mathematical core `nat_cubic_newton_lb` proving `a < ((a/x² + 2x)/3 + 1)³` via 3-variable two-equal AM-GM `(q + 2x)³ ≥ 27qx²` (`nat_cubic_amgm`) chained from 2-variable AM-GM `q² + x² ≥ 2qx` (multiplied by `q` and `x`), a manually-proved cube binomial expansion ladder (`nat_sq_expand`/`nat_cube_expand`/`nat_three_mul_cube`/`nat_two_y_cube`/`nat_2y1_cube`/`nat_succ_cube` substituting for unavailable `ring`), and an integer-only f64.cbrt() replacement `cbrt_guess_u64` composing `log2_floor_rec` (toNat = Nat.log2) + `pow2_loop` (toNat = g·2^(k−i)) to produce a power-of-two overestimate `g ≤ 2^22` carried into Newton's overflow discharge via `x/(g·g) ≤ g` (from `x ≤ g³`); the master `cbrt_postcondition` projects six contract clauses (totality, lower/upper Nat-level bounds, two boundary unit pins for cbrt 0 and cbrt 1).
